name: BoostRoyal
description: A consumer-focused game-boosting marketplace offering rank boosts, win boosts, coaching and smurf/account sales for competitive games (LoL, Valorant, CS, TFT, Wild Rift).

details.founded: Unknown (site states it has operated for over ten years)
details.targetMarket: Competitive PC and mobile gamers seeking fast rank progression, coaching, or pre-leveled (smurf) accounts across games like League of Legends, Valorant, CS2, TFT, Wild Rift.

items: Match-by-match delivery with estimated completion time
‘Undercover’ / stealth mode to reduce detection (win rates below 65%)
Priority handling and live-streamed boost options
Smurf / pre-leveled account sales across multiple games
Account filters (server, rank, skins, blue essence) and price tiers
Dynamic price calculator generating per-order quotes
Security measures claimed for account safety (VPN, offline mode)
Booster verification and internal review system (site-hosted reviews)

missingFeatures: No standard, comparable productized packages — pricing is per-order via calculator, which makes apples-to-apples comparison between boosters and services difficult for buyers
No third-party-verified booster identity or skill verification layer (reliance on internal reviews only) — opportunity for a verified expert/badge system
No subscription or recurring coaching packages with progress tracking — coaching appears sold as one-off sessions without persistent analytics or milestone tracking
Limited buyer-protection standardization (refunds and guarantees are vaguely worded) — clearer escrow, staged delivery, or SLA guarantees would reduce buyer risk
No public API or B2B offering for platforms / streamers to integrate boosting or coaching services
Minimal transparent outcome analytics — customers lack structured progress reports, video highlights, or measurable coaching KPIs after a service

pricingPlans.name: Per-service (dynamic)
pricingPlans.note: Prices are generated per-order via a dynamic calculator (varies by game, rank, server and options); some win boosts start around $2-$5.

pricingPlans.name: Accounts (marketplace)
pricingPlans.note: Smurf / pre-leveled accounts are sold with prices depending on rank, server and skins; examples on site show low-tier accounts starting in the low tens of dollars.

pricingEvidence.label: ELO boost / pricing explanation page
pricingEvidence.claim: BoostRoyal uses a dynamic calculator to generate per-order prices for ELO/rank boosting rather than fixed static price tables.

pricingEvidence.label: Marvel Rivals wins page (sample pricing)
pricingEvidence.claim: Shows example starting prices for 'wins' beginning at approximately $2-$5 at lower ranks.

pricingEvidence.label: Account marketplace listing (sample price)
pricingEvidence.claim: Marketplace account listings include low-tier examples (e.g., Minecraft account listed at $13), demonstrating account sales pricing.

companyEvidence.label: Company reviews page (self-reported metrics)
companyEvidence.claim: Site claims tens of thousands of reviews and a large order volume (mentions 65,000+ customer reviews and 250,000+ orders completed on the reviews page).

companyEvidence.label: Trustpilot company profile
companyEvidence.claim: BoostRoyal's Trustpilot profile shows thousands of reviews and a high TrustScore, supporting market reach and customer count.

companyEvidence.label: LinkedIn company entry
companyEvidence.claim: Public LinkedIn company entry exists (limited company metadata available), indicating a corporate presence online.

reviewEvidence.label: Trustpilot reviews
reviewEvidence.claim: Thousands of mostly positive reviews on Trustpilot, demonstrating high-volume customer feedback (BoostRoyal shows ~9k+ reviews on Trustpilot).

reviewEvidence.label: Reddit complaint thread
reviewEvidence.claim: User reports of failed or low-quality service and claims of being scammed — examples of negative customer experiences in public forums.

reviewEvidence.label: Facebook reviews / complaint post
reviewEvidence.claim: Public Facebook reviews include customer complaints about refunds and service quality, supporting mixed sentiment in some channels.

featureEvidence.label: LoL boosting product page
featureEvidence.claim: Describes ELO boost services including solo/duo/placement options, priority handling and security assurances.

featureEvidence.label: Loyalty program page
featureEvidence.claim: Documents BoostCoin loyalty rewards and tiered discounts for repeat customers.

featureEvidence.label: Contact / security mention
featureEvidence.claim: Support page highlights 24/7 LiveChat and states security measures used to protect customer accounts (VPN, offline mode referenced elsewhere on product pages).

strengths: Large, established footprint in the boosting niche with thousands of independent reviews (Trustpilot/Reviews.io), which builds buyer confidence relative to anonymous providers.
Wide service breadth: rank boosting, win boosting, coaching and an account marketplace across many popular competitive titles, enabling cross-sell and repeat customers.
Operational features for consumer convenience: 24/7 live chat, per-order dynamic pricing calculator, priority/streaming add-ons and a loyalty program to drive retention.
weaknesses: Pricing and packaging opacity — dynamic per-order quotes make direct price comparison and value benchmarking difficult for buyers.
Trust and verification gaps — reliance on internal reviews and site claims without a public, third-party booster verification or standardized skill badges.
Service quality inconsistency — public forum complaints and some refund disputes indicate variability in booster performance and support responsiveness.
Limited B2B/API capabilities and no clear subscription or long-term coaching programs for players wanting sustained progression and measurable outcomes.
